response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack software engineer with strong web, backend, and developer-tooling experience, but the role requires deep hardware performance expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator architecture, GPU workload analysis, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in the resume. Low-latency DLang systems work and brief C/C++ experience at National Instruments are the closest touchpoints, but they fall well short of the principal-level hardware architecture background this position demands.
strengths: 18+ years of software engineering experience, including staff-level roles at major tech companies
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching in DLang handling millions of requests/sec)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and knowledge sharing
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon architecture, or hardware performance engineering experience
No deep-learning training/inference optimization or GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack and product engineering, not hardware-software co-design

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or DL workload optimization background
